universe_source: SAMPLE DATA - run the workflow for real output

regime.note: valuation components in Pabrai/Damani are scored vs this median; 'cheap' is regime-dependent

grade_note: Any triggered hard veto caps grade at C. V1/V2/V4-V7 require manual verification (checklist on each card).
disclaimer: Educational purposes only. Not investment advice. Not SEBI-registered research. Framework scores are quantified proxies of published investor philosophies computed from reported data; qualitative judgment is not automated. Suggested weights are a risk-budget illustration, not a recommendation. Verify independently before acting.

portfolio.sizing_note: inverse-volatility weights, capped at 8% - risk illustration only

stocks.name: Cummins India Ltd. (SAMPLE)

manual_veto_checklist.V1: SEBI / ED / exchange action - verify on SEBI + NSE surveillance lists
manual_veto_checklist.V2: Promoter pledge > 50% - verify NSE shareholding pattern
manual_veto_checklist.V4: Qualified audit opinion - verify latest annual report
manual_veto_checklist.V5: Promoter stake drop > 10% in 12 months - verify shareholding history
manual_veto_checklist.V6: Related-party transactions - verify RPT disclosures in annual report; value siphoning via related entities destroys more Indian small/midcaps than pledges do
manual_veto_checklist.V7: Auditor change / resignation in last 24 months - verify NSE announcements; a resigning auditor is a fire alarm

bear_case: Weakest lens: Kacholia scores just 2.5/10 (3y growth + operating leverage + size runway)
Operating cash flow is DECLINING year-over-year while the score rests on reported profits
